questions.question: Improve: 'She told that she would come.' Best improvement:
options: She said that she would come
She spoke that she would come
She told me that she would come
She talked that she would come
questions.correctAnswer: She told me that she would come
questions.explanation: 'Told' needs an indirect object: 'told me'. Or use 'said' without object.

questions.question: Improve: 'He is too weak that he cannot walk.' Better:
options: He is so weak that he cannot walk
He is too weak to walk
He is very weak to walk

questions.question: Improve: 'She has been working in this company since five years.'
questions.explanation: 'Since' is for a specific point in time; 'for' is used for duration (five years).

questions.question: Improve: 'He is my cousin brother.'
questions.explanation: In standard English, 'cousin' alone suffices; 'cousin brother' is Indian English.

questions.question: Improve: 'I prefer tea over coffee.'
options: I prefer tea more than coffee

questions.question: Improve: 'Please do me a favor.'
options: Please do a favor for me
Please do the favor for me
questions.explanation: 'Do me a favor' is correct idiomatic English.
